description: This figure presents comprehensive TEM analysis of dislocation-κ-carbide interactions in fatigued Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel samples tested at stress amplitude Δσ/2 = 180 MPa. Panels (a) and (b) display bright-field TEM images comparing intragranular κ-carbides (indicated by green circles) and dislocation lines (blue arrows) in samples 550-6 (aged at 550°C for 6 h, ~11 nm precipitates) and 650-6 (aged at 650°C for 6 h, ~24 nm precipitates), respectively; both samples exhibit precipitates within the shearing regime rather than Orowan bypassing. Panels (c) and (d) show high-resolution TEM (HRTEM) images of κ-carbides interacting with dislocations, with (c') and (d') providing magnified views of the green-rectangled regions. The corresponding inverse fast Fourier transform (IFFT) filtered images (c'') and (d'') highlight κ-carbides (white), austenitic matrix (black), and transition regions (blue). Red arrows in (c'') and (d'') indicate shear steps and localized contrast variations within the carbides, evidencing disruption of the ordered L12 structure and formation of antiphase boundaries (APBs). These observations demonstrate that repeated dislocation shearing causes mechanical dissolution of κ-carbides during cyclic loading, supporting the critical role of precipitate size in fatigue deformation mechanisms.
